perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of the present Agreement, “Mater ial” is defined as any data, test, study or any information in support of the Registration under REACH made available to the Consortium or generated or determined or used in any way by the Consortium to comply with the requirements of REACH and provided to the Re ceiving Party for the purpose of registration under REACH. This Material shall include, without limitation: (i) Data and/or Studies acquired, licensed, developed or contracted or obligat ed for by the Consortium pursuant to Section 10 of the Consortium Agreement; (ii) Registration Dossi er - technical dossier comprising studies, including test results; study su mmaries; proposals for testing; classification and labelling; guidance on safe use; plus a chemical safety repo rt; and (iii) draft Registration Dossier, interim and working documents related to the prepar ation of the Registra tion Dossier, know-how, technical information, researches , methods, practices, procedures, processes, formulas and more generally any information with resp ect to substances that is made available, in written or oral form, to the Receiving Party by the Consortium and/or the Consortium Members. Unless defined herein, the definitions in this Agreemen t shall be the same as those set forth in the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of the present Agreement, “Material” refers to information provided to the Receiving Party. Unless defined herein, the definitions in this Agreement shall be the same as those set forth in the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed definition of 'Material,' including specific examples like 'Data and/or Studies,' and 'Registration Dossier', the amended definition becomes vague. Section 2 now refers to 'Material' without a clear understanding of what it encompasses beyond a general idea of 'information'. This creates uncertainty around what information is actually protected under the NDA, creating an in-text contradiction. In Section 2, the term Material is being reffered and it is ambigious, due to the perturbation that was made in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence a nd not to use the Material for any purpose other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement. In Particular, the Receiving Party undertakes (in addition and wit hout prejudice to any commitments under the Consortium Agreement), that: a. the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies; b. the Material shall neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplication have not been specifically authorized by the Consortium Agreement or otherwise approved in writing by the Steering Committee; The Receiving Party acknowledges that the covena nts of non-disclosure and non-use in this Agreement shall be effective in every country in the world. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the highest de gree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information. In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific definition of 'Material,' including examples like data, studies, and registration dossiers. The changed text omits this detailed definition, rendering the term 'Material' vague. The contradicted text relies on a clear understanding of what constitutes 'Material' to enforce obligations like confidentiality and non-use. The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction, because the receiving party's obligations become unclear and unenforceable due to the ambiguous nature of the word Material in the Changed text, thereby conflicting with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not be subject to the obligations of this Agreement with respect to Material which: a. is or becomes known publicly through no wr ongful act of the Receiving Party; or b. was already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure hereunder as shown by the Receiving Party’s prior written records; or c. is learned by the Receiving Party from a third party under no obligation to the Consortium; or d. is independently developed by an employee, ag ent, or consultant of the Receiving Party with no knowledge of disclosure hereunder; e. is approved for release by the Consortium Co mmittee in compliance with Article 119 of REACH on electronic public access with the decision for submission of a Registration Dossier; f. is approved for release by written authoris ation of the Steering Committee or the IMOA Executive Committee; or g. is publicly disclosed in accord ance with the provisions of Section 15 of the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall not be subject to the obligations of this Agreement with respect to Material which: a. is or becomes known publicly through no wr ongful act of the Receiving Party; or b. was already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure hereunder as shown by the Receiving Party’s prior written records; or c. is learned by the Receiving Party from a third party under no obligation to the Consortium; or d. is independently developed by an employee, ag ent, or consultant of the Receiving Party with no knowledge of disclosure hereunder;
perturbation.explanation: By removing exceptions 'e', 'f' and 'g', it creates an in-text contradiction because sections 'e','f', and 'g' outline situations when the Receiving Party *is* allowed to disclose the material if it is approved by the comittee or is compliant with a section in the agreement. By removing this it is contradicting and suggesting the receiving party is *never* allowed to disclose. Section 4 states that Material that is independently developed by the Receiving Party is exempt from nondisclosure, this contradicts Section 2, which requires all Material to be kept confidential, regardless of its origin.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence a nd not to use the Material for any purpose other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement. In Particular, the Receiving Party undertakes (in addition and wit hout prejudice to any commitments under the Consortium Agreement), that: a. the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies; b. the Material shall neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplication have not been specifically authorized by the Consortium Agreement or otherwise approved in writing by the Steering Committee; The Receiving Party acknowledges that the covena nts of non-disclosure and non-use in this Agreement shall be effective in every country in the world. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the highest de gree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information. In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes exceptions 'e', 'f', and 'g' which outlined situations where the Receiving Party *is* allowed to disclose the material if it is approved by the committee or compliant with a section in the agreement. By removing these exceptions, the changed text suggests the receiving party is *never* allowed to disclose, contradicting the contradicted text which states the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence. The removal of these exceptions creates a direct conflict with the conditions under which disclosure is permitted, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall survive until the earlier of (i) such time as all the material disclosed hereunder becomes public ly known pursuant to Section 15 of the Consortium Agreement and made generally available without vi olation of this Agreement, or (i i) twelve (12) years from date of Registration.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall survive for twelve (12) years from the date of Registration.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause '(i) such time as all the material disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known pursuant to Section 15 of the Consortium Agreement and made generally available without violation of this Agreement', the contract now states the agreement ends exactly 12 years after registration. This now contradicts section 4, where exceptions for non-disclosure are outlined. If a material becomes public during the 12 years, the new version of the contract fails to account for it, creating an in-text contradiction that could create enforcement issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall not be subject to the obligations of this Agreement with respect to Material which: a. is or becomes known publicly through no wr ongful act of the Receiving Party; or b. was already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure hereunder as shown by the Receiving Party’s prior written records; or c. is learned by the Receiving Party from a third party under no obligation to the Consortium; or d. is independently developed by an employee, ag ent, or consultant of the Receiving Party with no knowledge of disclosure hereunder;
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for the agreement to end before 12 years if the material became publicly known. The changed text removes this possibility, mandating the agreement last the entire 12 years regardless. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states the receiving party has no obligation if the material becomes public. The removal of the clause creates a contradiction because the Receiving party would still be held liable for 12 years, even if the material was already made public as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and all dis putes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved in accordance with the laws of England and Wales.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'and all disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved in accordance with', the agreement lacks specific guidance on *how* disputes are to be resolved (e.g., arbitration, mediation, litigation). This creates ambiguity. The original text clearly defines that the agreement will be under the laws of England and Wales, removing the second half is contradicting the first because it is inferring the resolution is still under those laws, when there is no resolution outlined. Because of this, this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that disputes would be resolved in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. The changed text removes the part specifying how disputes are to be resolved, creating an ambiguity as to the method. The contradicted text states the agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales. While it's the same as the modified text, the omission in the changed text creates a contradiction, as it implies the agreement would follow procedures under those laws, when there is no resolution outlined. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction, as defined by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
